name: cpu_crypto_chacha
title: ChaCha20 encryption
short_description: ChaCha20-Poly1305 authenticated encryption throughput on all cores.

structured_text.description: Encrypts and authenticates a buffer with ChaCha20-Poly1305 repeatedly across all cores. This cipher is built from plain arithmetic rather than dedicated instructions, so it shows how a processor handles secure traffic when no hardware cipher acceleration is in play, which is common on lighter and mobile hardware.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Throughput is the number of bytes encrypted and authenticated per second, reported in GB/s.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. The gap against the AES result shows how much a machine relies on dedicated cipher instructions versus general arithmetic.
structured_text.reasoning: ChaCha20-Poly1305 is widely used for secure connections, especially where hardware AES is weak or absent, so it captures a different and important side of cryptographic performance.

domain_gaming.explanation: Games use authenticated encryption mainly for network traffic.

domain_office.explanation: Secure browsing and messaging often run on this cipher.

domain_software_development.explanation: Secure transfers and tunnels frequently negotiate this cipher.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Design work touches authenticated encryption only when syncing.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Secure media delivery sometimes uses this cipher.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Rendering does almost no encryption work.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work uses encryption only for transfers.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering work encrypts mainly when protecting files in transit.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Secure links between nodes can use this cipher for data movement.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Training pipelines spend little time on transport encryption.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Serving over secure connections uses authenticated encryption per request.

domain_data_science.explanation: Pulling data over secure links runs this cipher.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Servers terminate many secure connections, often on this cipher for clients without hardware AES.
